disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Established prior diagnosis of a chronic energy-limiting (ME/CFS-type) illness with a recognised severe post-exertional 'crash' pattern the patient has experienced before.
trigger_question_examples: Can you tell me a bit about your health background?
Has anything like this happened to you before?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I've had this ME / chronic fatigue thing for years. This is one of my bad crashes — but it's the worst in a long time and I'm on my own.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No fever, chills or sweats, no urinary frequency/dysuria, no cough or other new infective symptoms (screens for occult infection/sepsis).
trigger_question_examples: Do you have any fever, chills or sweats?
Any burning or stinging when you pass urine, or going more often?
Any cough, sore throat or other signs of an infection?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, no fever or chills, no burning when I wee, nothing like that. It's just the crash.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No black/tarry or bloody stools, no vomiting of blood, no new marked pallor or breathlessness at rest (screens for GI bleed / severe anaemia).
trigger_question_examples: Have you noticed any black, tarry or bloody bowel motions?
Have you vomited any blood?
Do you look unusually pale, or feel breathless even just resting?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, nothing like that — no blood, my bowels are normal, and I'm not breathless lying here.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No drooping eyelids, double vision, slurred speech, or trouble swallowing/breathing, and no one-sided face/arm weakness; the heaviness is generalised and constant rather than fatiguable bulbar weakness (screens myasthenic crisis / stroke).
trigger_question_examples: Any drooping eyelids, double vision, or trouble swallowing or chewing?
Is your speech slurred, or is one side of your face or body weaker than the other?
Does the weakness get worse the more you use a muscle, then come back after resting?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, my face and speech are fine and both sides feel the same — I'm just weak and heavy all over, not on one side.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Markedly reduced oral intake for about 48 hours with an inability to physically reach or lift fluids; reduced urine output; dry mouth — markers of evolving dehydration the patient cannot self-correct.
trigger_question_examples: Have you been able to drink and eat?
When did you last have a proper drink, and are you passing urine as normal?
Are you able to reach and hold a glass of water yourself?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I can barely reach my water and I can't lift a full bottle. I've hardly had anything to drink and I don't think I've passed much urine today.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Lives alone with no one present to help; strictly bedbound tonight with a high fall risk; cannot perform basic self-care including reaching fluids.
trigger_question_examples: Is there anyone at home with you, or who could come and help?
Are you able to get up safely, or are you stuck in bed?
Do you have someone who can check on you tonight?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I live on my own and there's nobody here. I can't get up safely — I'd fall — so I'm stuck in bed.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Severe orthostatic intolerance — marked dizziness and a pounding/racing heartbeat on sitting up, forcing the patient to stay flat.
trigger_question_examples: What happens when you try to sit or stand up?
Do you feel dizzy or lightheaded, or does your heart race, when you change position?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: As soon as I try to sit up I get really dizzy and my heart pounds, so I have to lie straight back down.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No chest pain and no breathlessness at rest; the only palpitation is the positional fast heartbeat (screens an acute cardiac cause for the tachycardia).
trigger_question_examples: Do you have any chest pain or pressure?
Are you short of breath even while lying still?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No chest pain, and my breathing's okay when I'm lying flat — it's just when I sit up that my heart races.
